response.content:
# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

request.stdin: SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.tool_type') AS tool_type, json_extract(properties,'$.length') AS length, json_extract(properties,'$.grip_type') AS grip_type FROM products WHERE brand='Bahco' AND series='Bahco Heavy Duty S' AND model='ETS-3CM' AND name='Pliers and Wrenches' AND json_extract(properties,'$.tool_type')='adjustable wrench' AND json_extract(properties,'$.length')='250 mm' AND json_extract(properties,'$.grip_type')='ergonomic' LIMIT 10;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type') AS fastener_type, json_extract(properties,'$.diameter') AS diameter FROM products WHERE brand='Heco' AND series LIKE '%Heco Zinc Plated%' AND model='HECO 3DW-64B' AND name='Nut Bolt and Washer' AND json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt' AND json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='10 mm' LIMIT 10;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.tool_type') AS tool_type, json_extract(properties,'$.length') AS length, json_extract(properties,'$.grip_type') AS grip_type FROM products WHERE brand='Bahco' AND name='Pliers and Wrenches' AND (series LIKE '%Bahco Heavy Duty S%' OR model LIKE '%ETS-3CM%' OR series LIKE '%Heavy Duty%' OR model LIKE '%ETS%') LIMIT 50;

SELECT sku, path, json_extract(properties,'$.diameter') AS diameter FROM products WHERE brand='Heco' AND series LIKE '%Heco Zinc Plated%' AND model='HECO 3DW-64B' AND name='Nut Bolt and Washer' AND json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt' LIMIT 20;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.tool_type') AS tool_type, json_extract(properties,'$.length') AS length, json_extract(properties,'$.grip_type') AS grip_type FROM products WHERE brand='Bahco' AND (model LIKE '%ETS-3CM%' OR series LIKE '%Bahco Heavy Duty S%' OR series LIKE '%Heavy Duty%' OR model LIKE '%ETS%') LIMIT 100;

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.

2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

{"sku":"SFE-1RHZ96B3","category_id":"safety_gear","kind_id":"respiratory_protection","family_id":"fam_safety_gear_respiratory_protection_0002_jede4jmo","brand":"Moldex","series":"Comfort","model":"Classic MDR-T27","name":"Moldex Comfort Classic MDR-T27 Respiratory Protection filter cartridge basic one size","price_cents":1900,"price_currency":"EUR","properties":{"mask_type":"filter cartridge","protection_class":"basic","size":"one size","standard":"EN 149"}}

request.message: <YES> — this exact catalogue variant exists: Moldex Comfort Classic MDR-T27 Respiratory Protection with mask type "filter cartridge" and protection class "basic".

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS cnt, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT p.path) AS paths
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id = i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id = 'adhesives_glues'
  AND s.city = 'Graz'
  AND s.is_open = 1
  AND i.available_today > 0;

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS cnt
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id = i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id = 'wall_paint'
  AND s.city = 'Graz'
  AND s.is_open = 1
  AND i.available_today > 0;

FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id = i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id = 'wood_drywall_screws'
  AND s.city = 'Linz'
  AND s.is_open = 1
  AND i.available_today > 0
ORDER BY p.path;

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

response.stdout:
name,sql
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"

request.stdin:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS cnt
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id = i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id = 'automotive_cleaners'
  AND s.city = 'Vienna'
  AND s.is_open = 1
  AND i.available_today > 0;

WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT id, name FROM stores
  WHERE lower(name) LIKE '%brno%' AND lower(name) LIKE '%veveri%'
  LIMIT 5
), targets AS (
  SELECT 1 AS item_no, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Heco'
    AND p.category='Nut Bolt and Washer'
    AND p.series='Heco Zinc Plated'
    AND p.model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type')='threaded rod'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 2, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Ajax'
    AND p.category='Cleaning Liquid'
    AND p.series='Ajax Eco Classic'
    AND p.model='25C-DXE'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.cleaner_type')='degreaser'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 3, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Heco'
    AND p.category='Nut Bolt and Washer'
    AND p.series='Heco Zinc Plated'
    AND p.model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='10 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 4, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Festool'
    AND p.category='Tool Box and Bag'
    AND p.series='Festool Stackable'
    AND p.model='SYS 3JJ-9LM'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.storage_type')='tool bag'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 5, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Heco'
    AND p.category='Nut Bolt and Washer'
    AND p.series='Heco Zinc Plated'
    AND p.model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='8 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 6, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Ajax'
    AND p.category='Cleaning Liquid'
    AND p.series='Ajax Eco Classic'
    AND p.model='25C-DXE'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.cleaner_type')='floor cleaner'
)
SELECT ts.id AS store_id, ts.name AS store_name, t.item_no, t.sku, t.path, i.available_today
FROM target_store ts
CROSS JOIN targets t
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.store_id=ts.id AND i.sku=t.sku
ORDER BY t.item_no;

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT id, name, path FROM stores
  WHERE lower(name) LIKE '%brno%' AND lower(name) LIKE '%veveri%'
  LIMIT 5
), targets AS (
  SELECT 1 AS item_no, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Heco'
    AND p.series='Heco Zinc Plated'
    AND p.model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND p.name='Nut Bolt and Washer'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type')='threaded rod'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 2, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Ajax'
    AND p.series='Ajax Eco Classic'
    AND p.model='25C-DXE'
    AND p.name='Cleaning Liquid'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.cleaner_type')='degreaser'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 3, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Heco'
    AND p.series='Heco Zinc Plated'
    AND p.model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND p.name='Nut Bolt and Washer'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='10 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 4, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Festool'
    AND p.series='Festool Stackable'
    AND p.model='SYS 3JJ-9LM'
    AND p.name='Tool Box and Bag'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.storage_type')='tool bag'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 5, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Heco'
    AND p.series='Heco Zinc Plated'
    AND p.model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND p.name='Nut Bolt and Washer'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='8 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 6, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Ajax'
    AND p.series='Ajax Eco Classic'
    AND p.model='25C-DXE'
    AND p.name='Cleaning Liquid'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.cleaner_type')='floor cleaner'
)
SELECT ts.id AS store_id, ts.name AS store_name, ts.path AS store_path, t.item_no, t.sku, t.path, i.available_today
FROM target_store ts
CROSS JOIN targets t
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.store_id=ts.id AND i.sku=t.sku
ORDER BY t.item_no;

SELECT sku,path,brand,series,model,name,properties FROM products WHERE brand IN ('Bondex','Philips','Geberit','Heco','Bahco') AND (model IN ('106-TS1','3JS-MSN','IC5-GU6','2VD-VNA','1ZR-PGS') OR series LIKE '%Garden Classic%' OR series LIKE '%Professional Hue%' OR series LIKE '%Mapress Silent%' OR series LIKE '%Unix HECO%' OR series LIKE '%Comfort Grip BE%' OR name IN ('Wood Stain and Deck Oil','LED Bulb','Valve and Connector','Nut Bolt and Washer','Pliers and Wrenches')) LIMIT 200;

    AND p.model='SYS 3JJ-9LM'

  UNION ALL
  SELECT 5, p.sku, p.path

    AND p.model='HECO 3DW-64B'
    AND p.name='Nut Bolt and Washer'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='8 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 6, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Ajax'
    AND p.series='Ajax Eco Classic'
    AND p.model='25C-DXE'
    AND p.name='Cleaning Liquid'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.cleaner_type')='floor cleaner'
)
SELECT t.item_no, t.sku, t.path, i.available_today
FROM targets t
LEFT JOIN inventory i

WITH lend_store AS (
  SELECT id, name FROM stores
  WHERE name LIKE '%Lend%'
), candidates AS (
  SELECT 'c1' AS cid, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  JOIN lend_store s ON s.id=i.store_id
  WHERE p.brand='Bosch'
    AND p.category='Automotive Charger and Bulb'
    AND p.series='Bosch Workshop'
    AND p.model='GWS 17A-FAQ'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.product_type')='jump starter'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'c2', p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  JOIN lend_store s ON s.id=i.store_id
  WHERE p.brand='Carhartt'
    AND p.category='Work Trousers'
    AND p.series='Carhartt Stretch Rugged'
    AND p.model='3EY-11K'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Green'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.size')='L'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fit')='relaxed'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'c3', p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  JOIN lend_store s ON s.id=i.store_id
  WHERE p.brand='Shell'
    AND p.category='Automotive Charger and Bulb'
    AND p.series='Shell Professional Advance'
    AND p.model='1PO-W67'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.product_type')='battery charger'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.voltage')='12 V'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.current')='6 A'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'c4', p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  JOIN lend_store s ON s.id=i.store_id
  WHERE p.brand='Carhartt'
    AND p.category='Work Trousers'
    AND p.series='Carhartt Stretch Rugged'
    AND p.model='3EY-11K'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Black'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.size')='M'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fit')='regular'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'c5', p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  JOIN lend_store s ON s.id=i.store_id
  WHERE p.brand='Keter'
    AND p.category='Shelving and Cabinet'
    AND p.series='Keter Workshop Connect'
    AND p.model='3K5-2SL'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.storage_type')='workshop cart'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Black'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'c6', p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p
  JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku
  JOIN lend_store s ON s.id=i.store_id
  WHERE p.brand='Moldex'
    AND p.category='Respiratory Protection'
    AND p.series='Moldex Comfort Classic'
    AND p.model='1HX-XWP'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.mask_type')='disposable respirator'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.protection_class')='ffp2'
)
SELECT (SELECT id FROM lend_store LIMIT 1) AS store_id,
       cid, sku, path, available_today,
       CASE WHEN available_today>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS qualifies
FROM candidates
ORDER BY cid;

inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (

	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,

products,"CREATE TABLE products (

	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,

)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (

WITH meidling AS (
  SELECT id FROM stores WHERE id='store_vienna_meidling'
), targets AS (
  SELECT 1 AS ord, 'bondex_wood_stain' AS label, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Bondex' AND p.series='Garden' AND p.model='Classic 106-TS1'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Wood Stain and Deck Oil%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.product_type')='wood stain'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 2,'philips_led_10w_470lm', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Philips' AND p.series='Professional' AND p.model='Hue 3JS-MSN'
    AND p.name LIKE '%LED Bulb%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.wattage')='10 W'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.luminous_flux')='470 lm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 3,'geberit_shower_hose_a', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Geberit' AND p.series='Mapress' AND p.model='Silent IC5-GU6'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Valve and Connector%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.connector_type')='shower hose'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 4,'geberit_shower_hose_b', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Geberit' AND p.series='Mapress' AND p.model='Silent IC5-GU6'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Valve and Connector%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.connector_type')='shower hose'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 5,'heco_bolt_12_40_500', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Heco' AND p.series='Unix' AND p.model='HECO 2VD-VNA'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Nut Bolt and Washer%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='12 mm'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.length')='40 mm'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.pack_count')='500'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 6,'bahco_side_cutter_125', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Bahco' AND p.series='Comfort Grip' AND p.model='BE 1ZR-PGS'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Pliers and Wrenches%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.tool_type')='side cutter'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.length')='125'
)
SELECT t.ord,t.label,t.sku,t.path,i.available_today
FROM targets t
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=t.sku AND i.store_id=(SELECT id FROM meidling)
ORDER BY t.ord;

WITH lend_store AS (
  SELECT id, path, name FROM stores
  WHERE city='Graz' AND name LIKE '%Lend%'
  LIMIT 1
), c1 AS (
  SELECT 'c1' cid, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku JOIN lend_store s ON s.id=i.store_id
  WHERE p.brand='Bosch' AND p.series='Bosch Workshop' AND p.model='GWS 17A-FAQ'
    AND p.name='Automotive Charger and Bulb'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.product_type')='jump starter'
), c2 AS (
  SELECT 'c2' cid, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku JOIN lend_store s ON s.id=i.store_id
  WHERE p.brand='Carhartt' AND p.series='Carhartt Stretch Rugged' AND p.model='3EY-11K'
    AND p.name='Work Trousers'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Green'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.size')='L'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fit')='relaxed'
), c3 AS (
  SELECT 'c3' cid, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku JOIN lend_store s ON s.id=i.store_id
  WHERE p.brand='Shell' AND p.series='Shell Professional Advance' AND p.model='1PO-W67'
    AND p.name='Automotive Charger and Bulb'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.product_type')='battery charger'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.voltage')='12 V'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.current')='6 A'
), c4 AS (
  SELECT 'c4' cid, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku JOIN lend_store s ON s.id=i.store_id
  WHERE p.brand='Carhartt' AND p.series='Carhartt Stretch Rugged' AND p.model='3EY-11K'
    AND p.name='Work Trousers'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Black'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.size')='M'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fit')='regular'
), c5 AS (
  SELECT 'c5' cid, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku JOIN lend_store s ON s.id=i.store_id
  WHERE p.brand='Keter' AND p.series='Keter Workshop Connect' AND p.model='3K5-2SL'
    AND p.name='Shelving and Cabinet'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.storage_type')='workshop cart'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Black'
), c6 AS (
  SELECT 'c6' cid, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
  FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=p.sku JOIN lend_store s ON s.id=i.store_id
  WHERE p.brand='Moldex' AND p.series='Moldex Comfort Classic' AND p.model='1HX-XWP'
    AND p.name='Respiratory Protection'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.mask_type')='disposable respirator'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.protection_class')='ffp2'
), allc AS (
  SELECT * FROM c1 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM c2 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM c3
  UNION ALL SELECT * FROM c4 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM c5 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM c6
)
SELECT (SELECT id FROM lend_store) AS store_id,
       (SELECT path FROM lend_store) AS store_path,
       cid, sku, path, available_today,
       CASE WHEN available_today>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS qualifies
FROM allc ORDER BY cid;

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

categories,"CREATE TABLE categories (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	department TEXT NOT NULL
)"
customers,"CREATE TABLE customers (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
	email TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	home_lat REAL NOT NULL,
	home_lon REAL NOT NULL
)"
employee_roles,"CREATE TABLE employee_roles (
	employee_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	role TEXT NOT NULL,
	PRIMARY KEY (employee_id, role),
	FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employees(id)
)"
employees,"CREATE TABLE employees (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
	email TEXT NOT NULL,
	title TEXT NOT NULL,
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
)"
families,"CREATE TABLE families (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
payment_lines,"CREATE TABLE payment_lines (
	payment_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	line_no INTEGER NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	unit_price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	PRIMARY KEY (payment_id, line_no),
	FOREIGN KEY (payment_id) REFERENCES payments(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
payments,"CREATE TABLE payments (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_archived INTEGER NOT NULL,
	customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	amount_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	status TEXT NOT NULL,
	created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
	payment_method_fingerprint TEXT NOT NULL,
	device_fingerprint TEXT NOT NULL,
	observed_lat REAL NOT NULL,
	observed_lon REAL NOT NULL,
	three_ds_status TEXT,
	three_ds_failure_reason TEXT,
	three_ds_attempts INTEGER,
	three_ds_max_attempts INTEGER,
	FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
)"
product_kinds,"CREATE TABLE product_kinds (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL
)"
product_properties,"CREATE TABLE product_properties (
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	key TEXT NOT NULL,
	value_text TEXT NOT NULL,
	value_number REAL,
	PRIMARY KEY (sku, key)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
returns,"CREATE TABLE returns (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	payment_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	status TEXT NOT NULL,
	reason_code TEXT NOT NULL,
	created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
	FOREIGN KEY (basket_id) REFERENCES baskets(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (payment_id) REFERENCES payments(id)
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"

request.stdin:
WITH s AS (
  SELECT id, path FROM stores WHERE city='Graz' AND name LIKE '%Lend%' LIMIT 1
),
c1 AS (
  SELECT 'c1' cid, p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Bosch' AND p.series='Workshop' AND p.model='GWS 17A-FAQ'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Automotive Charger and Bulb%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.product_type')='jump starter'
  LIMIT 1
),
c2 AS (
  SELECT 'c2' cid, p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Carhartt' AND p.series='Stretch' AND p.model='Rugged 3EY-11K'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Work Trousers%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color')='Green'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.size')='L'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fit')='relaxed'
  LIMIT 1
),
c3 AS (
  SELECT 'c3' cid, p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Shell' AND p.series='Professional' AND p.model='Advance 1PO-W67'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Automotive Charger and Bulb%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.product_type')='battery charger'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.voltage_v')=12
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.current_a')=6
  LIMIT 1
),
c4 AS (
  SELECT 'c4' cid, p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Carhartt' AND p.series='Stretch' AND p.model='Rugged 3EY-11K'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Work Trousers%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color')='Black'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.size')='M'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.fit')='regular'
  LIMIT 1
),
c5 AS (
  SELECT 'c5' cid, p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Keter' AND p.series='Workshop' AND p.model='Connect 3K5-2SL'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Shelving and Cabinet%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.storage_type')='workshop cart'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color')='Black'
  LIMIT 1
),
c6 AS (
  SELECT 'c6' cid, p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Moldex' AND p.series='Comfort' AND p.model='Classic 1HX-XWP'
    AND p.name LIKE '%Respiratory Protection%'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.mask_type')='disposable respirator'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.protection_class')='ffp2'
  LIMIT 1
),
allc AS (
  SELECT * FROM c1 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM c2 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM c3
  UNION ALL SELECT * FROM c4 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM c5 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM c6
)
SELECT a.cid, a.sku, a.path, i.available_today,
       CASE WHEN i.available_today>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS qualifies,
       (SELECT id FROM s) AS store_id,
       (SELECT path FROM s) AS store_path
FROM allc a
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=a.sku AND i.store_id=(SELECT id FROM s)
ORDER BY a.cid;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type') AS screw_type FROM products WHERE brand='Heco' AND name='Wood and Drywall Screw' AND series LIKE '%Heco Zinc Plated TopFix GTU-YPJ Wood and Drywall Screw%' AND json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type')='drywall screw' LIMIT 10;
SELECT id, path, name, city FROM stores WHERE city='Bratislava';
SELECT sku, properties FROM products WHERE sku IN ('WRK-2C1DPQAV','WRK-2C46MVO8','STO-UAMOIAXC') ORDER BY sku;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type') AS screw_type FROM products WHERE brand='Heco' AND (name LIKE '%Wood%' OR name LIKE '%Drywall%' OR series LIKE '%TopFix%' OR series LIKE '%GTU-YPJ%') LIMIT 50;
